rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing output features.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared variables matching AADL data types.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts should avoid implementation-specific subcomponent ports.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq declarations for boolean range checks with proper type alignment.
Place implementation-level assign statements in component implementation scopes.
Ensure all visible identifiers in AGREE expressions are declared in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Avoid using opaque Boolean data in numeric range comparisons.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints
Model fusion and scope preservation rules
